title: High-quality processes and rare nonconformities
text: IQCC distinguishes two corrected methods: type = "cf1" uses first skewness correction; type = "cf2" uses two-adjustment operational limits documented Joekes Barbosa (2013). following chunk executed compares three limit methods.
code: methods <- c("normal", "cf1", "cf2") p_results <- do.call(   rbind,   lapply(methods, function(method) {     lim <- pchart_limits(p = 0.015, n = 20, type = method)     risk <- pchart_alpha_risk(       p = 0.015,       n = 20,       lcl = lim$lcl,       ucl = lim$ucl     )     data.frame(       method = method,       lcl = lim$lcl,       center = lim$center,       ucl = lim$ucl,       actual_alpha = risk,       arl0 = ifelse(risk == 0, Inf, 1 / risk)     )   }) ) p_results #>   method lcl center        ucl actual_alpha       arl0 #> 1 normal   0  0.015 0.09653924 0.0357458712   27.97526 #> 2    cf1   0  0.015 0.16120479 0.0002023458 4942.03542 #> 3    cf2   0  0.015 0.13031923 0.0031780828  314.65511 data(binomdata) cchart.p(   x1 = binomdata$Di[1:12],   n1 = binomdata$ni[1:12],   type = "cf2",   x2 = binomdata$Di[13:25],   n2 = binomdata$ni[13:25] )

title: High-quality processes and rare nonconformities
text: DS-np plan first-stage count D1 second-stage count D2, IQCC uses following rule: accept first stage D1 <= floor(wl); signal first stage D1 >= floor(ucl1) + 1; otherwise inspect second sample; second sample, accept D1 + D2 <= floor(ucl2); signal second stage otherwise.

title: Improved Quality Control Charts
text: Univariate control charts: X-bar, R, S, p, u charts. Improved probability limits: exact, Cornish-Fisher corrected, standardized, simulation-based limits. High-quality processes: corrected p charts double-sampling np charts rare nonconformities. Multivariate monitoring: Hotelling T² charts, generalized variance charts, auxiliary tr(V) variability charts. False-alarm diagnostics: exact binomial, Poisson, range-chart, generalized variance risk calculations available. Phase Phase II support: retrospective estimation prospective monitoring. Important methodological themes include: Cornish-Fisher quantile correction highly skewed attribute statistics; exact discrete false-alarm evaluation binomial Poisson charts; exact range-chart limits relative range distribution; double-sampling designs rare nonconformities; Hotelling T² monitoring multivariate process means; generalized variance monitoring products chi-square variables Bartlett decomposition; auxiliary tr(V) monitoring trace standardized Wishart matrix.

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: x1 Integer vector nonconforming counts first sample. n1 First-stage sample size (positive integer). n2 Second-stage sample size (positive integer). p0 -control nonconforming proportion. x2 Optional integer vector nonconforming counts second sample. Must length x1 provided. Use NA observations second sample taken. Required observations x1 falls intermediate (warning) zone. wl Fractional warning limit. Must less ucl1. ucl1 Fractional upper control limit first stage. Must greater wl. ucl2 Fractional upper control limit combined samples. limits Optional object returned dsnp_limits(). provided, wl, ucl1, ucl2 taken limits$best. alpha Maximum desired false alarm probability p0. Used limits need computed via dsnp_limits(). p1 Optional --control proportion. provided, performance metrics p1 included returned object. plot Logical. TRUE (default), draws control chart. FALSE, returns result object. ... Additional arguments passed plot().

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: object class "cchart.DSnp", list following elements: call matched call. data data.frame columns index, x1,     x2, total, stage, signal. limits list fractional integer thresholds:     wl, ucl1, ucl2, wl_accept,     ucl1_reject, ucl2_accept. parameters list n1, n2, p0,     alpha, p1. performance list -control performance metrics     (arl0, ass0, p_signal0), optionally     --control metrics (arl1, ass1,     p_signal1) p1 provided.

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: DS-np chart uses two sampling stages. first stage, sample size n1 inspected. count x1 warning limit, process accepted. x1 exceeds first upper control limit, process signals --control immediately. Otherwise, second sample size n2 inspected combined count x1 + x2 compared second upper control limit. Limits can supplied manually via wl, ucl1, ucl2, obtained pre-computed dsnp_limits() object via limits argument, computed automatically inside function neither provided. fractional limits converted integer thresholds using convention numerical core functions: wl_accept = floor(wl): accept first stage         x1 <= wl_accept. ucl1_reject = floor(ucl1) + 1: signal first stage         x1 >= ucl1_reject. ucl2_accept = floor(ucl2): accept second stage         x1 + x2 <= ucl2_accept.

code: set.seed(123) phase1 <- array(rnorm(6 * 8 * 2), dim = c(6, 8, 2))  # Known in-control covariance, exact dimension-two limits. chart <- cchart.GV(     phase1,     Sigma = diag(2),     type = "exact",     plot = FALSE ) chart$limits[c("lcl", "center", "ucl")] #> $lcl #> [1] 0 #>  #> $center #> [1] 0.8571429 #>  #> $ucl #> [1] 4.621594 #>  summary(chart) #> Generalized Variance Control Chart #>   Dimension: p = 2 ; subgroup size n = 8  #>   Subgroups: 6 (Phase I: 6 ; Phase II: 0 ) #>   Limits: exact / upper ; nominal alpha = 0.0027  #>   Covariance: supplied Sigma  #>   LCL = 0 ; center = 0.8571 ; UCL = 4.622  #>   Signals: 0   # Estimate |Sigma| from Phase I and evaluate two Phase II subgroups. phase2 <- array(rnorm(2 * 8 * 2), dim = c(2, 8, 2)) estimated <- cchart.GV(     phase1,     x2 = phase2,     type = "cf",     plot = FALSE ) estimated$phase2 #> [1] 7 8

T2II is a vector with the value of the first T2 statistic. cchart.T2.2(T2II, 50, 1, 1, 25, 2)  # Same of the above, but now showing the phase I data set. cchart.T2.2(T2II, 50, 1, 1, 25, 2, datum = datum)

title: Trace Statistic for Multivariate Variability — trv_stat
text: statistic complements generalized variance \(|S|\). Two covariance matrices can determinant different trace standardization \(\Sigma_0\); trv_stat() designed make structural change visible. independent sampling multivariate normal process covariance \(\Sigma_0\), $$(n - 1)S \sim W_p(n - 1, \Sigma_0)$$ therefore $$(n - 1)\operatorname{tr}(\Sigma_0^{-1}S) \sim       \chi^2_{p(n - 1)}.$$ implementation evaluates trace (n - 1) * sum(Sigma0_inverse * S), avoiding explicit matrix square root preserving value.
